justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Jurisdiction-specific rules identified for all 3 jurisdictions — California wage transparency/AB5, Germany works council/BetrVG, Indonesia contractor terminology. (2) Germany works council approval identified as a blocking pre-condition via BR-4 and AC-3 with feature flag gate. (3) Driver classification data model difference explicitly identified in BR-5 and AC-6 (employee vs independent contractor per jurisdiction). (4) Formal escalation for legal review present in multiple escalations and business_impact.risk. (5) No single global acceptance criteria set — ACs are jurisdiction-specific. (6) confidence.level = 52, well below the 60 threshold.

observations.text: Exceptional depth on California Labor Code §226(a) requirements with specific statutory references
BR-4 correctly models works council approval as a system-enforced gate with testable assertion on feature flag
BR-8 captures the subtle Indonesia terminology trap — avoiding employment-implying language to prevent reclassification risk
BR-5 correctly identifies driver classification as explicit jurisdiction-level configuration, never inferred
Edge cases are exceptionally thorough — driver relocation, approval revocation, negative net pay, midnight minimum wage change, and race conditions
Business impact correctly identifies phased jurisdiction-specific rollout requirement and 3-6 month Germany delay
Confidence at 52 with well-articulated low-confidence areas including BetrVG scope uncertainty and missing tax withholding requirements
Escalation on works council rejection strategy shows strategic thinking beyond the immediate spec
Correctly identifies the subtle trap that removing transparency in California would be illegal even if simplifying globally
Could have more explicitly stated 'cannot produce a single acceptance criteria set' as a top-level finding rather than letting it emerge implicitly from jurisdiction-specific ACs

grader_results.detail: field escalations[*].type equals check failed: expected 'ambiguity', got values: 'missing_context'
